mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer demand that lifts corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a contraction in industrial production indicates capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that force immediate equity repricing, causing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of late-cycle months when INDPRO declines, or the signal shows zero predictive power in a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250, inflationary pressures force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 250, or the threshold effect vanishes in a quantile regression test.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total employment and falling unemployment confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant at the 90% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into immediate positive equity repricing, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 returns following each data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters following a positive GDPC1 revision, or the event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, simultaneous rebounds in industrial output and retail sales confirm demand-supply synchronization that drives positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of recovery-phase observations where INDPRO exceeds 105, or the regime-specific predictive accuracy drops below 50%.
